zh.lifeSummary: 當John III Savage於大約1401年出生在英國爾克里夫頓時，他的父親John V Sir Savage32歲，母親Maud Swynnerton32歲。他於1422年與Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton在英國英格蘭爾克里夫頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他在英國爾馬可萊斯費爾德被列為bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england。他去世於1463年6月29日在英國英格蘭爾克里夫頓享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾馬可萊斯費爾德聖 米可艾爾也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John III Savage于大约1401年出生在英国爾克里夫頓时，他的父亲John V Sir Savage32岁，母亲Maud Swynnerton32岁。他于1422年与Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton在英国英格兰爾克里夫頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他在英国爾馬可萊斯費爾德被列为bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england。他去世于1463年6月29日在英国英格兰爾克里夫頓享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾馬可萊斯費爾德聖 米可艾爾也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John III Savage was born about 1401, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, his father, John V Sir Savage, was 32 and his mother, Maud Swynnerton, was 32. He married Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton in 1422, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 7 daughters. His occupation is listed as bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england in Macclesfield, Cheshire, England. He died on 29 June 1463, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton en 1422, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 7 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england à Macclesfield, Cheshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 29 juin 1463, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: John III Savageが約1401年にイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで生まれた時，父John V Sir Savageは32歳，母Maud Swynnertonは32歳であった。1422年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンでEleanor  Elizabeth Breretonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。イギリスチェシュアイアマククレスフィールドでの職業はbailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, englandと記録されている。彼は1463年6月29日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアマククレスフィールドスト ミチャエル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Michael's and All Angels Churchyard,: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 맥클스필드 스트 믹하얼'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: John III Savage(이)가 대략 1401년 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John V Sir Savage(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Swynnerton(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1422년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 클리프턴에서 Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 체실르 맥클스필드에서 bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england이었다. 그는 1463년 6월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 클리프턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 맥클스필드 스트 믹하얼'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton em 1422, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 7 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england em Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra. ru.lifeSummary: Когда John III Savage родился около 1401 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John V Sir Savage было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Swynnerton, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton в 1422 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 7 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england в местности макклесфиелд, чешир, Англия. Он умер 29 июня 1463 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мачаел'с чурч, макклесфиелд, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton en 1422, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 7 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era bailiff of the forest of macclesfield, cheshire, england en Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 29 de junio de 1463, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
